profile.name: Abdul Samad Zeeshan
profile.thesis: LLM outputs grounded in deterministic verification layers.
profile.headline: I build reliable AI and software systems.
profile.intro: I design systems where AI outputs are validated through schemas, tests, evaluation harnesses, and deterministic checks, so the final result is not just impressive but dependable.
profile.relocation: Open to relocating anywhere in Canada.
profile.education: BSc Computing Science, University of Alberta, 2026

projects.label: Docket
projects.oneLiner: An event-driven serverless pipeline that turns receipt PDFs into schema-checked JSON, all AWS CDK in TypeScript, with a CI-gated accuracy eval on every push.

metrics.label: Field-level accuracy
metrics.note: Over a 42-receipt synthetic golden set (exact match plus line-item F1), CI-gated at 0.90. Real recorded model responses over synthetic receipts.

metrics.label: Model-call latency
metrics.note: The Bedrock model call only, n=42, real percentiles.

metrics.label: Cost per receipt
metrics.note: 311 input and 182 output tokens, from the model's own token counts.

metrics.label: Tests
metrics.note: Includes CloudFormation template assertions and cdk-nag. One LocalStack integration test is skipped by design.

metrics.label: Live deploy
metrics.note: Deployed to a live AWS account and validated with a real receipt (2.82s end to end, n=1), then torn down. It never ran in production.

projects.label: Triage-0.6B
projects.oneLiner: A fine-tuned 0.6B model that triages support tickets into structured JSON at 87% priority accuracy on 1,200 held-out tickets versus 38% for the base model, running offline on a consumer GPU.

metrics.label: Priority accuracy
metrics.note: On 1,200 held-out tickets, base model versus the fine-tuned model.

metrics.label: Model
metrics.note: LoRA fine-tuning plus knowledge distillation from a DeepSeek teacher.

metrics.label: Serving
metrics.note: Exported to GGUF and served locally through LM Studio on an RX 6750 XT (Vulkan). No inference-time API.

projects.label: Tarn
projects.oneLiner: An identity-security lakehouse over a billion real login events. PySpark batch, a dbt star schema, Redpanda streaming, and Neo4j privilege-path graphs, with the negative results committed alongside the wins.

metrics.label: Events processed
metrics.note: Real anonymized LANL authentication events over 58 days, counted and sha256-verified, not quoted.

metrics.label: Rollup
metrics.note: Across 80,553 identities, joined to 749 labelled red-team events.

metrics.label: Spark optimization
metrics.note: 50.696s to 39.378s median on a 113.7M-row slice. This is scale-dependent, and the baseline wins on the small CI slice. All four variants produce byte-identical output, checked by CRC32.

metrics.label: Streaming
metrics.note: p50 12.54s and p95 15.57s end-to-end lag, which includes a deliberate 2-minute watermark hold. This is an accelerated local replay, not a production feed.

metrics.label: Graph blast radius
metrics.note: Compromised identities against a benign control. 1.9x reach at 1 hop, and a ratio of 1.0 (both about 98% of hosts) by 3 hops.

metrics.label: Vector search
metrics.value: 406x retrieval lift, loses as a detector
metrics.note: As a detector it loses to four simple SQL rules. As retrieval from a known-bad day it reaches precision@10 of 4.59% against a 0.0113% base rate.

metrics.label: CI
metrics.note: The tests, the dbt build, five analytical queries, and the vector stage all run on every push.

projects.label: Change-Gate
projects.oneLiner: An agentic change-approval gate that scores risk deterministically before any model judgment, exposed as an OAuth-guarded MCP server with a hash-chained audit trail.

metrics.label: Tests
metrics.note: 5 Postgres-gated skips, matching CI. They cover the risk engine, auth primitives, hash-chained audit, and agent graph, not the MCP transport.

metrics.label: MCP tools exposed
metrics.note: Registered as @mcp.tool() over streamable HTTP behind an OAuth 2.1 resource server.

metrics.label: Scoring purity
metrics.note: A purity test fails the build if a scoring module imports anthropic, openai, langchain, langgraph, or httpx.

projects.label: Fulcrum
projects.oneLiner: Hybrid RAG/CAG retrieval that builds UAlberta degree plans, with a FAISS cosine index, a CP-SAT constraint solver, and a citation audit over every retrieved span.

metrics.label: Courses scraped
metrics.note: Accuracy is never claimed over the full 954.

metrics.label: Eval scope
metrics.note: A SHA-1-hashed evaluation harness over a hand-verified gold set.

metrics.label: Retrieval index
metrics.note: Cosine similarity over L2-normalised span embeddings. This is an in-process vector index, not a hosted vector database.

projects.label: Touchstone
projects.oneLiner: An LLM tutoring platform where a tested Python oracle owns every number and grade. The model only rewords prose, and the app runs fully without it.

metrics.label: Backend tests
metrics.note: Runs in CI, with a live demo.

metrics.label: Line coverage
metrics.note: On application code. Core computation modules sit at 98 to 100%. The low modules are the LLM and TTS layers, which is what oracle-first predicts.

metrics.label: Datastore
metrics.note: A stateless backend. A tested Python oracle owns every number and grade.

projects.label: Tally
projects.oneLiner: A banking service on a double-entry ledger with exactly-once idempotent transfers, backed by 254 JUnit tests including real-Postgres integration.

metrics.label: Backend tests
metrics.note: Unit and real-Postgres integration, in CI on every push.

metrics.label: Web tests
metrics.note: React and TypeScript client.

metrics.label: Correctness
metrics.note: Backed by a DB unique constraint. Money is stored as integer minor units, and postings are balanced so they net to zero.

projects.label: Kvasir
projects.oneLiner: An in-memory key-value store in Go, a goroutine-per-connection TCP server whose shutdown path is designed to be race-free and exercised under Go's race detector.

metrics.label: Race detector
metrics.note: The whole suite passes under go test -race, including TestConcurrentClients (8 clients x 50 ops) and TestShutdownClosesClients.

metrics.label: Dependencies
metrics.note: A goroutine-per-connection TCP server over a custom line-based protocol.

metrics.label: Shutdown
metrics.note: One shared critical section covers connection admission and drain, so a connection admitted mid-shutdown cannot outlive the WaitGroup.

projects.label: Pathfinder
projects.oneLiner: A C++17 pathfinding engine with four algorithms behind one interface, checked by cross-algorithm oracle tests and shipped as an install-free WebAssembly demo.

metrics.label: Algorithms
metrics.note: BFS, Dijkstra, Greedy Best-First, and A*, behind one interface.

metrics.label: Verification
metrics.note: Each algorithm is checked against the others on the same graphs.

metrics.label: Demo
metrics.note: Compiled with Emscripten to an install-free browser demo.

edges.evidence: The fine-tuning and evaluation pipeline is written in Python.
Fine-tuned Qwen3-0.6B with LoRA. Ticket-priority accuracy rose from 38% to 87% on 1,200 held-out tickets.
Distilled from a DeepSeek teacher model into the 0.6B student.
87% priority accuracy versus 38% for the base model, measured on 1,200 held-out tickets.
The agent, deterministic risk engine, and MCP server are written in Python.
LangGraph runs a fixed fetch, validate, assess, decide, explain DAG. Deterministic risk scoring runs before any model judgment.
Exposes nine change-and-risk tools as an MCP server on the official Python SDK over streamable HTTP, with a matching MCP client.
An OAuth 2.1 resource server with JWKS-verified bearer tokens, per-tool scopes, and a second scope required to record a decision.
Postgres row-level security. A local run reported 79 passed with 5 Postgres-gated skips, matching CI.
79 tests cover the risk engine, auth primitives, hash-chained audit, and agent graph. A purity test fails the build if a scoring module imports a model client.
The 79-test suite runs in GitHub Actions on every push.
An optional explainer calls Claude to turn a decision into prose. The model never affects the score, band, or decision.
The retrieval, constraint solver, and evaluation harness are written in Python.
Hybrid RAG/CAG retrieval over 954 scraped courses, with a citation audit over every retrieved span.
FAISS IndexFlatIP over L2-normalised span embeddings, which is cosine similarity by inner product. Every query resolves through the FAISS index.
Course-catalogue spans are embedded and L2-normalised before indexing.
A SHA-1-hashed evaluation harness is scoped to 31 hand-verified courses, so accuracy is never claimed over the full 954.
A CP-SAT constraint solver assembles a valid term-by-term degree plan under prerequisite and course-load constraints.
React and TypeScript front end with hand-rolled SVG widgets.
React and TypeScript client for interactive lessons where intuition fails.
FastAPI and Pydantic backend. A tested Python oracle owns every number and grade.
Oracle-first, so the tested code does all computation and the model only rewords prose. Answers are never model-invented.
202 backend tests with over 90% line coverage on application code. Core computation modules sit at 98 to 100%.
The 202-test suite runs in CI. The app is deployed and has a live demo.
An in-memory key-value store written from scratch in Go using the standard library only.
A goroutine-per-connection TCP server. A Store interface guarded by sync.RWMutex allows concurrent reads and serialized writes.
The whole suite, including TestConcurrentClients (8 clients x 50 ops) and TestShutdownClosesClients, passes under go test -race.
The entire pipeline and its infrastructure are written in TypeScript.
S3 to EventBridge to SQS and DLQ to a Lambda to DynamoDB, plus an IAM-authed HTTP API. Deployed once to a live account and validated end to end, then torn down.
All infrastructure defined as AWS CDK in TypeScript, with 111 tests including CloudFormation template assertions and cdk-nag.
An event-driven serverless pipeline with a DLQ (maxReceiveCount 3), idempotent writes, and PII redaction before anything is stored.
Zod-schema-gated LLM extraction with one repair retry and FAILED-versus-DLQ error routing.
Extraction runs Claude Haiku 4.5 on Bedrock. CI replays recorded responses so it never makes a live model call.
A field-level eval harness over a 42-receipt golden set (exact match plus line-item F1), CI-gated at 0.90, scores 0.999.
Idempotent conditional writes keyed on sha256(bucket/key/etag) prevent double-processing.
CloudWatch dashboard and alarms to SNS, X-Ray active tracing, and an AWS Budgets guard at $10 per month.
111 tests and the accuracy eval run in GitHub Actions. Deploys use a GitHub OIDC role with no long-lived keys.
An HTTP API with IAM auth is backed by a query Lambda and a DynamoDB status GSI.
Java backend on the JDK HTTP server with virtual threads and hand-written JSON.
React and TypeScript web client over the ledger API.
React and TypeScript client for accounts and transfers.
A PostgreSQL double-entry ledger. Transfers are balanced postings that net to zero, re-derivable through a reconciliation endpoint.
Exactly-once idempotent transfers backed by a DB unique constraint. A client fault toggle demonstrates a retried lost response with no double-pay.
Pessimistic row locks at read committed, money stored as integer minor units, decisions recorded as ADRs.
A hand-written JSON HTTP API sits over the double-entry ledger.
254 backend JUnit tests (unit and real-Postgres integration) plus 31 web vitest tests.
The 254 JUnit and 31 vitest tests run in CI on every push.
An extensible C++17 pathfinding engine behind one object-oriented Pathfinder interface.
BFS, Dijkstra, Greedy Best-First, and A* implemented behind a shared interface.
Shipped as an install-free browser demo compiled to WebAssembly with Emscripten.
Cross-algorithm oracle tests check each algorithm against the others on the same graphs.
The PySpark batch layer, dbt models, and FAISS indexing are written in Python.
PySpark batch over 1,051,430,459 real anonymized authentication events, parsed into a 58-partition Parquet lake.
Reduced the event lake to 1,604,500 identity-days across 80,553 identities with window functions, joined to 749 labelled red-team events.
Spark Structured Streaming from Redpanda with 1-minute tumbling windows, a 2-minute watermark, and a checkpoint kill-and-resume recovery test. It sustained 7,139 events per second.
A DuckDB star schema built with dbt. Fact and dimension models carry unique, not_null, and relationships tests on every key, all green in CI.
A Neo4j graph of 72,751 users, 14,540 computers, and 600,145 auth edges answers shortest-path and blast-radius questions in Cypher.
A FAISS HNSW index over 1,604,500 behavioural vectors. As retrieval it reaches precision@10 of 4.59% against a 0.0113% base rate, a 406x lift.
Each person-day is encoded as a standardised 52-dimension behavioural vector, with anti-leakage enforced by a test.

Signals were scored against 181 labelled attack days with a benign control group. The honest negatives (off-hours share caught 0 of 181, blast radius saturates past one hop) are committed, not hidden.
33 tests, a dbt build of 45 checked resources, five analytical queries, and the vector stage all run in GitHub Actions CI.
The full pipeline, dbt build, queries, and vector stage run in CI. The demo publishes only after a site audit passes.
